bugs.error_message: PowerShell reports: cannot load C:\Users\Admin\Documents\WindowsPowerShell\Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1 because script execution is disabled
bugs.root_cause: PowerShell execution policy blocks the user profile script, causing every shell command to emit noisy errors.
bugs.fix: Not fixed in this review; use powershell -NoProfile for commands or adjust/remove the profile after user approval.

bugs.error_message: AGENTS.md references @RTK.md, but D:\zhihu\zhihu\RTK.md was not found
bugs.root_cause: The project instruction file points to a missing root-level RTK.md include.
bugs.fix: Not fixed; recorded for handoff because Windows Run Owner should not alter project instruction structure without user direction.

bugs.error_message: python zhihuTTS.py validation commands timed out in the runner after preprocessing/Gemini phases
bugs.root_cause: The full daily batch can run longer than the tool timeout; one run was interrupted during Gemini send and a retry later proved cache reuse.
bugs.fix: No code change on Windows. Captured the timeout in runs/windows-whispercpp-validation-20260516.md and verified retry reused cache before completing the video.

bugs.error_message: git worktree add /private/tmp/zhihu-stream-validation feature/stream-transcript-validation failed with: fatal: could not create leading directories of '.git/worktrees/zhihu-stream-validation': Operation not permitted
bugs.root_cause: Sandbox blocked Git from creating worktree metadata under the repository .git directory.
bugs.fix: Reran the same git worktree command with explicit user-approved escalation; temporary worktree was created successfully.

bugs.error_message: Mac hardware cannot support the complete replay-stream validation run
bugs.root_cause: The full replay source is about 02:42:53 long, and local CPU Whisper validation is expected to take multiple hours on Mac.
bugs.fix: Stopped Mac-side full validation and created a Windows handoff with branch, ffprobe result, and command instructions.

bugs.error_message: pre-commit rejected mixed commit: avoid mixing code/collaboration docs with runs/*.md
bugs.root_cause: The staged change mixed OpenWolf memory files with a new runs/*.md Windows handoff report.
bugs.fix: Unstaged the mixed change and split into separate commits.

bugs.error_message: Whisper audio transcription quality is poor for the completed stream replay transcript
bugs.root_cause: The current stream replay runner uses the Whisper-compatible backend, which produced lower-quality Chinese lecture transcripts than the Windows SenseVoice probe.
bugs.fix: Not fixed yet. Analyze Windows SenseVoice validation and add FunASR SenseVoice as an optional backend with Whisper fallback and domain glossary normalization.

bugs.error_message: Windows report says stream slicing retry code and sensevoice_probe.py were used, but pushed branch contains only reports and no corresponding code files
bugs.root_cause: Validation code appears to have stayed local on Windows or was not included in the pushed commits.
bugs.fix: Fixed by Windows commit 5371c70, which added sensevoice_probe.py and hardened zhihuTTS_stream.py slice retries.

bugs.error_message: python3 -m py_compile - failed with: No such file or directory
bugs.root_cause: Used py_compile with '-' as if it accepted source code from stdin; py_compile treats '-' as a file path.
bugs.fix: Re-ran syntax checks by loading git show output in python and calling compile(source, filename, 'exec').

bugs.error_message: git fetch origin failed with: error: cannot open '.git/FETCH_HEAD': Operation not permitted
bugs.root_cause: Default workspace sandbox prevented updating Git fetch metadata.
bugs.fix: Re-ran git fetch origin with approved escalated permissions; fetch completed successfully.

bugs.error_message: python -m json.tool .wolf/buglog.json failed with: zsh:1: command not found: python
bugs.root_cause: This environment exposes Python as python3, not python.
bugs.fix: Re-ran validation with python3 -m json.tool .wolf/buglog.json; JSON parsed successfully.

bugs.error_message: git worktree prune failed with Operation not permitted; git branch --force failed to create .git refs lock with Operation not permitted
bugs.root_cause: Default workspace sandbox blocked Git metadata writes while clearing stale worktree state and updating a local branch ref.
bugs.fix: Re-ran git worktree prune and git branch --force with approved escalated permissions; local feature/stream-transcript-validation now matches origin at ddd77a4.

bugs.error_message: Code review found potential abnormal Google API consumption on feature/stream-transcript-validation
bugs.root_cause: Wrapper scripts pass --gemini by default; Windows BAT also invokes scripts/build_stream_markdown.py afterward when GEMINI_API_KEY is set, duplicating full-stream Gemini synthesis. Retry/continuation limits can further multiply calls.
bugs.fix: Partially fixed first issue on stream branch worktree: removed --gemini from run_zhihu_live.bat's zhihuTTS_stream.py invocation so Windows BAT keeps scripts/build_stream_markdown.py as the single final NotebookLM Gemini synthesis path. Remaining recommendations: gate other wrapper defaults behind explicit flags, add max-call/max-frame budgets, and avoid retrying deterministic non-rate-limit errors.

bugs.error_message: _call_gemini_stream() silently returns {text: None} when all retries exhausted — no log message
bugs.root_cause: Outer retry loop fell through without printing a final failure message; callers had no visibility that synthesis was abandoned
bugs.fix: Added stderr print before final return: 'All N retries exhausted — Gemini synthesis failed.'

bugs.error_message: --cleanup-slices only deleted slice audio/video files; per-slice RUNS_DIR intermediate files accumulated indefinitely
bugs.root_cause: cleanup_slices block only called slice_path.unlink(); did not remove transcript_path, global_transcript_path, payload_path, report_path
bugs.fix: Added cleanup block after write_report(): when args.cleanup_slices, unlink all 4 per-slice RUNS_DIR files

bugs.error_message: git show ... | jq ... failed with: zsh:1: command not found: jq
bugs.root_cause: This Mac environment does not have jq installed.
bugs.fix: Used Node.js JSON.parse with git show output to summarize A/B transcript and payload artifacts without installing dependencies.

bugs.error_message: sed: stream_extractors.py: No such file or directory
bugs.root_cause: Current working tree is on main with URL/live stream files partially present, but stream_extractors.py only exists on feature/stream-transcript-validation.
bugs.fix: Read stream_extractors.py from feature/stream-transcript-validation with git show for analysis instead of treating the current dirty main worktree as complete.

bugs.error_message: subprocess.TimeoutExpired: ffmpeg slice timed out after 240s when stream ended — StreamEndedError never raised
bugs.root_cause: slice_url() used duration*3+60 timeout (240s for 60s chunk); TimeoutExpired propagated as-is, bypassing is_stream_ended() check path.
bugs.fix: Fixed 2026-05-21: timeout changed to duration+45 (105s); TimeoutExpired caught and re-raised as StreamSliceError so caller's is_stream_ended() fires. Committed to feature/stream-transcript-validation.

bugs.error_message: merge_stream_chunks.py and build_stream_markdown.py only processed 1 of 157 chunks — used max(groups.keys()) timestamp grouping
bugs.root_cause: extract_run_ts() grouped each chunk by its per-chunk completion timestamp; max() picked the single latest group (1 chunk out of 157).
bugs.fix: Fixed 2026-05-21: removed defaultdict grouping from default path; --run-ts flag preserved as explicit override. Committed to feature/stream-transcript-validation.

bugs.error_message: --max-chunks ignored in --hls-consumer-only mode (processed all .ts files regardless of cap)
bugs.root_cause: run_hls_consumer_only() directly collected all seg_*.ts files without checking args.max_chunks. The max_chunks cap logic only existed in the Recorder flow.
bugs.fix: Added args.max_chunks check before processing: if set and len(ts_files) > args.max_chunks, truncate ts_files to first N files with a console notice.

bugs.root_cause: Code path had no error handling — exceptions would propagate uncaught
bugs.fix: Added try/catch block

bugs.error_message: User reports sectioned live synthesis would spend about 20 Gemini API requests for a 180-minute live stream, violating CLAUDE.md quota constraints
bugs.root_cause: The experimental sectioned Pass A/B/C pipeline was exposed through the production live final CLI without enforcing the project's one-call default or request budget controls.
bugs.fix: Removed the production --sectioned route so build_stream_markdown.py remains the one-shot live synthesis path; marked live_sectioned_synthesis.py usage text experimental pending a budget-compliant design.

bugs.error_message: rg: unrecognized flag --sectioned|run_full_pipeline\(|Usage \(experimental|Gemini API Free-Tier Limits|Prefer one Gemini
bugs.root_cause: The ripgrep pattern began with --sectioned and was parsed as an rg option.
bugs.fix: Reran ripgrep with -- before the pattern so --sectioned is treated as search text.

bugs.error_message: rg: unrecognized flag -
bugs.root_cause: The roadmap checklist search pattern started with a dash and ripgrep parsed it as an option.
bugs.fix: Stopped relying on that failing search; the roadmap sections and progress table were already read with line numbers for review.

bugs.error_message: build_stream_markdown.py: 105 chunks → 105 groups → only 1 chunk selected; Transcript: 320 chars (should be 37,749); Frames: 2 (should be 335)
bugs.root_cause: extract_run_ts() groups chunks by individual completion timestamps. Each chunk has unique timestamp → 105 groups → max() picks most-recent single-chunk group instead of all chunks.
bugs.fix: When --run-ts not specified, use all found chunk files sorted by chunk start time. When --run-ts specified, filter by that timestamp with error listing available timestamps.

bugs.error_message: SyntaxWarning: invalid escape sequence '\m' in merge_stream_chunks.py line 9 docstring
bugs.root_cause: Docstring usage example used Windows backslash path; Python 3.12+ warns on unrecognized escape sequences in string literals.
bugs.fix: Changed backslash to forward slash in Windows usage example in docstring.

bugs.error_message: Missing error handling in check_markdown_body_coverage
bugs.root_cause: Code path had no error handling — exceptions would propagate uncaught
bugs.fix: Added try/catch block

bugs.error_message: merge_stream_chunks.py default path selects only 1 chunk from 105 (same root cause as bug-058)
bugs.root_cause: Default branch used max(groups.keys()) to pick the group with the latest per-chunk completion timestamp; for a 105-chunk live stream each chunk has a unique ts, so max() selected the single last chunk's group.
bugs.fix: When --run-ts absent: skip group selection, use sorted(all_found, key=parse_chunk_start). Added multi-group warn. Mirrors build_stream_markdown.py:448.

bugs.error_message: check_markdown_body_coverage() only printed to stdout; body coverage gap not persisted to final-qc.json
bugs.root_cause: Function returned None and was called after prepend_quality_header; coverage data never added to manifest or QC JSON so source_status=full masked a 610s tail truncation.
bugs.fix: Return type changed to dict (body_last_chapter_end_s, body_tail_gap_s, body_coverage_status). Call moved before prepend_quality_header. Manifest updated with coverage fields + body_tail_coverage_low warning. qc_path re-written post-Gemini.

bugs.error_message: Significant refactor of
bugs.root_cause: 4 lines replaced/restructured
bugs.fix: Rewrote 8→11 lines (4 removed)

bugs.error_message: merge_stream_chunks.py warning text misled operators to use --run-ts, which resolves per-chunk timestamps not session ids
bugs.root_cause: Warning said 'pass --run-ts to restrict' but --run-ts matches per-chunk completion timestamp; on a 105-chunk live run every chunk has a unique ts so following the hint reduces merge back to 1 chunk. Warning also dumped all 105 ts groups as noise.
bugs.fix: Single-line warn explaining all-chunks stop-gap, multi-session mixing risk, and explicit note that --run-ts is not a live session selector until run-manifest lands.

bugs.error_message: body_coverage_status='no_headings' not persisted to manifest warnings; Markdown header showed no anomaly
bugs.root_cause: main() only appended warning for status=='warning'; 'no_headings' branch was missing so warnings[] stayed empty and header rendered nothing.
bugs.fix: Added elif for 'no_headings': appends body_coverage_unverifiable to manifest warnings. Added renderer in prepend_quality_header.

bugs.error_message: WIN reported naming rule from 94b2a71 did not propagate to final Gemini output: BAT produced Markdowns/TTS_stream-1.md instead of TTS_stream-live_YYYYMMDD_<title>.md
bugs.root_cause: run_zhihu_live.bat generated a fallback NAME and always passed --name to zhihuTTS_stream.py, so Python's title/date naming fallback never ran. The BAT then used the stale NAME for merge_stream_chunks.py and build_stream_markdown.py. Additionally, the non-continuous live path in run_validation would have used replay_<title> when --name was omitted.
bugs.fix: Added --base-marker to zhihuTTS_stream.py and write_base_marker() to persist the resolved base_stem. run_validation now chooses live_YYYYMMDD_<title> when live_mode is true and no explicit --name is provided. run_zhihu_live.bat no longer passes --name in auto mode, passes --base-marker, reads the marker after transcription, and uses the resolved NAME for merge and final Gemini synthesis.

bugs.error_message: run_zhihu_live.bat could trigger duplicate/default-on Gemini synthesis: zhihuTTS_stream.py --gemini notes plus final build_stream_markdown.py NotebookLM generation
bugs.root_cause: BAT set GEMINI_FLAG=--gemini whenever GEMINI_API_KEY existed, so the stream transcription phase could call Gemini before the final NotebookLM synthesis. The final synthesis also had high retry/continuation defaults and no CLI dry-run/budget controls.
bugs.fix: Removed default --gemini from the live transcription phase. Added --no-gemini and --dry-run to run_zhihu_live.bat. BAT now prints a Gemini budget and only calls build_stream_markdown.py for the final one-shot document, passing --max-retries 2 and --max-continuations 2. build_stream_markdown.py now exposes --max-retries, --max-continuations, --dry-run, and prints its Gemini budget before any API call.

bugs.error_message: Local review commands hit environment/tooling errors: sandbox denied git fetch writing .git/FETCH_HEAD, sandbox denied npx gitnexus analyze writing ~/.gitnexus/registry.json, and jq was not installed
bugs.root_cause: Workspace sandbox blocks writes to .git and ~/.gitnexus without approval; jq is not available in the local shell environment.
bugs.fix: Retried git fetch and npx gitnexus analyze with approved escalation; replaced jq JSON summarization with a node stdin parser.

bugs.error_message: 2026-05-22 live URL-slice validation accumulated about 13 minutes of unrecorded time after aligning live transcript to replay transcript
bugs.root_cause: The validated live run used sequential 60s URL slicing. Each chunk recorded 60s, then spent processing/transcription time before starting the next slice while the real live stream continued. Manifest shows 105 chunks recorded 6300s but summed elapsed was 7082.69s, about 782.69s of overhead.
bugs.fix: Use the continuous HLS recorder/consumer architecture for real live streams, or otherwise decouple recording from transcription so processing overhead cannot create capture gaps. Keep URL-slice mode for replay/non-live only or mark it as lossy for real-time live capture.

bugs.error_message: Live final Markdown quality depended only on Gemini body attention and Windows live runner still used lossy sequential URL-slice capture by default
bugs.root_cause: The Windows live runner did not opt into continuous HLS, so real-time live capture could accumulate gaps while processing chunks. The final live Markdown also lacked deterministic transcript and visual evidence appendices, so Gemini body omissions directly reduced NotebookLM coverage.
bugs.fix: Changed run_zhihu_live.bat to pass --continuous-hls by default without stream-stage --gemini. run_continuous_hls now creates a per-run HLS work directory and records it in checkpoints/manifests. build_stream_markdown.py now appends full transcript and a visual evidence index after the Gemini body, and adds --mock-gemini-text for offline end-to-end validation without API calls.
